BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Quick back-of-napkin for Mike Crawford Consulting LLC. Pre-year-end review with the accountant. Sanity check that the environmental impact consulting side-business still covers its home-office allocation and the sporadic subcontracted GIS hours, with the CBMRI salary as the steady backbone (not modeled here).

CurrentState.Note: Rough estimate only. Need to revisit with the accountant after 2025 actuals are closed and 1099s come back from the GIS subcontractor.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent increases to $750/mo if property tax passes through
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $2,670 + $100 increase = $2,770. At $95/member, $2,770 / $95 = 29.2 → 30 members minimum.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Well within buffer at 55 members.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - rent to $850/mo (unlikely per Iverson)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Iverson said this was unlikely but wanted to stress test.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even is ~2-3 retainer clients at the current $850/mo rate. At 3 active retainers (Pew Charitable Trusts, Maine DEP, and the rotating small-org contract) we have comfortable headroom. Main risk is summer dip when fieldwork crowds out consulting hours - even then, the CBMRI base salary backstops everything.
ActionItems: Wait for the accountant to finalize the home-office allocation worksheet
Revisit after Q4 2025 actuals close, with realistic prorated mortgage interest and utilities
